item.title: Cowboy Heaven, inMOMA’s Westerns Series
item.description: Also: the third-wave emo of Jimmy Eat World, Jean Genet’s “The Maids” TikTokified, Rachel Syme’s shoe of the summer, and more.

item.title: Cote and the Risks of the Clubstaurant
item.description: With a soaring new complex in midtown, the restaurateur Simon Kim continues to turn his Korean-barbecue-meets-steak-house concept into a high-status luxury chain.
item.dc:creator: ByHelen Rosner

item.title: For the Nation’s Birthday, Making It Harder to Become an American
item.description: The Trump Administration has chosen to honor the Semiquincentennial of a nation of immigrants with a vision that sends the country back in time.
item.dc:creator: ByJonathan Blitzer

item.title: From Sturgeon to Knicks Gear, Barney Greengrass Has You Covered
item.description: The Upper West Side smoked-fish establishment, beloved by Marilyn Monroe and Irving Berlin, represents the best of New York—great food, family bonds, and Jalen Brunson.
item.dc:creator: ByDavid Remnick

item.title: Michael Arden Catches a Flight
item.description: The theatre director’s latest show, “The Lost Boys,” has its actors dangle high above the stage. At an indoor-skydiving joint, it’s his turn to defy gravity.
item.dc:creator: ByZach Helfand

item.title: Inside the Creative Cavern of JR
item.description: Riding in his custom-designed car on the Orient Express, the French artist reflects on his latest large-scale project—turning the Pont Neuf into a giant inflatable cave.
item.dc:creator: ByLauren Collins

item.title: Kurt Vile Gets the Feeling
item.description: On the heels of his tenth album, “Philadelphia’s Been Good to Me,” the musician catches a show at Le Poisson Rouge and reminisces on how Beck and Pavement ignited his passion for live music.
item.dc:creator: ByNick Paumgarten

item.title: For People with Misophonia, Everyday Noises Can Be Agony
item.description: The neurophysiological disorder is characterized by a severe aversion to sound—and the struggle to convince others of the severity of that aversion.
item.dc:creator: BySloane Crosley

item.title: Could Switzerland Become the First Country to Cap Its Population?
item.description: The Swiss will soon go to the polls for a novel initiative that could upend the nation’s economy and rupture ties with the European Union.
item.dc:creator: ByJessi Jezewska Stevens

item.title: Andrew Tate’s Empire of Abuse
item.description: How the defining figure of the manosphere built a fortune—and became a political force—by systematically exploiting women.
item.dc:creator: ByHeidi Blake

item.title: Ticks to Fear This Summer
item.description: Physically resembling a crossbreed of a gremlin and Stephen Miller’s soul, the Gray-Legged Tick can be found wherever it is even moderately interesting to vacation in the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: ByJay Ruttenberg

item.title: A Dad Story, or, After Six Beers
item.description: One man’s naval adventure, fuelled by hops.
item.dc:creator: ByRoz Chast

item.title: “Constellation”
item.description: There was something absurd and distressing in that prelude to the domestic drama.
item.dc:creator: ByAndrea Bajani

item.title: Did a Rowdy English Nobleman Mastermind the American Revolution?
item.description: America’s fight for independence is often considered a battle fought and won at home. A new book argues that it was propelled by a transnational élite an ocean away.
item.dc:creator: ByAdam Gopnik

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “The Lost Soldiers,” “Homebound,” “Once Upon a Time There Was Truth,” and “My World Is Melting.”

item.title: A World-Class Omakase in America’s Most Landlocked State
item.description: For the chef David Utterback, the sense that Omaha is underestimated is a source of both pride and torment.
item.dc:creator: ByHannah Goldfield

item.title: Edmonia Lewis, the Misunderstood Sculptor Who Brought Stone to Life
item.description: She was a Black and Indigenous woman who became famous for working in white marble. But the focus on her race can obscure the subtle, often contradictory triumphs of her art.
item.dc:creator: ByZachary Fine

item.title: The Rise of the Meta-Gay Show
item.description: In “Can I Be Frank?” and “Heated Rivalry: The Unauthorized Musical,” gay fandom generates funny and moving new material.
item.dc:creator: ByEmily Nussbaum

item.title: “Still Life of Mom in Her Garden (Bodegón de Mamá en Su Jardín)”
item.description: “It is not about the vegetables.”
item.dc:creator: ByIsabella DeSendi

item.title: “Watching Cartoons on a Saturday Morning”
item.description: “Cuckoo wobble, tuba walk, /boing boingand the whistling thribble.”
item.dc:creator: ByChristian Wiman

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the June 15, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Monday, June 8, 2026
item.description: A challenging puzzle.
item.dc:creator: ByPatrick Berry
